frameworks.name: neurovisceral integration
frameworks.one_line: shared prefrontal-autonomic network links HRV to cognitive and affective regulation

frameworks.name: central autonomic network
frameworks.one_line: CNS structures regulating cardiovascular output also support executive function

core_question: How do individual differences in heart rate variability relate to prefrontal neural function and executive-function performance?
key_assumptions: a shared neural network governs cognitive, affective, and autonomic regulation
HRV indexes prefrontal cortical inhibitory control over cardiovascular activity
executive-function tasks recruit the same prefrontal substrates that modulate HRV
manipulations: task type: executive- vs nonexecutive-function tasks
key_findings: individual HRV differences correlate with executive-function task performance
HRV tracks prefrontal cortical activity in healthy participants
manipulating resting HRV produces changes in executive-function performance
interpretations: HRV serves as a peripheral marker of prefrontal regulatory capacity
the cognition-HRV-prefrontal triad has implications for physical and mental health
